Targeted demethylation of the BRD7 promoter based on CRISPR/dCas9 system inhibits the malignant progression of nasopharyngeal carcinoma

evidence_entities.text: There was hypermethylation modification in BRD7 promoter, which was negatively correlated with BRD7 expression.

we constructed a LentiCRISPRv2/dCas9-TET1CD-sgRNAs system targeting specific methylation sites of BRD7 promoter based on five sgRNAs, and confirmed that all five sgRNA-guided CRISPR/dCas9 systems could activate BRD7 and inhibit cell proliferation to varying degrees, among which sgRNA2&sgRNA5 were the most significant.

both sgRNA2&sgRNA5 could promote the transcriptional activation by reducing its methylation, and inhibit the cell proliferation, migration, invasion and tumour growth in vivo of NPC, and the combination of them has a more significant demethylation, transcriptional activation and anti-tumour effect.

The growth curve of the subcutaneous tumours indicated that stable expression of dCas9-TET1CD-sgRNA2 and dCas9-TET1CD-sgRNA5 markedly suppressed tumour growth in vivo when compared to the control group, and the tumour weights were also considerably reduced relative to those of the control group.

the tumour volume and weight in the LV-dCas9-TET1CD-sgRNA2 and sgRNA5 groups were significantly reduced, with tumour suppression rates of 68% and 79%, respectively. Among them, the combination group exhibited the most pronounced suppression effect, with a tumour suppression rate of 90%.
